root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Product Overview Guide
root.summary: Front matter and opening section of the Cúram 8.1.2 Product Overview Guide. It identifies the guide scope (applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2), then introduces Cúram as a configurable social enterprise platform for human services, health, labor, social security, and military/veterans programs supporting Beveridge and Bismarck models. The section outlines core architecture and platform capabilities: social programs data model, intelligent evidence gathering, decision assist, financial management, funded program management, supervisor workspace, integrated case management, intake, eligibility and entitlement, evidence management, participant management, and major modules such as Appeals, BI&A, Evidence Broker, Provider Management, Universal Access, Verification Engine, and Income Support. It also covers technical architecture (ADE, runtime/development architecture, business infrastructure), tablet support, deprecated and removed functionality, and PDF access/prebuilt PDFs. The final pages contain legal notices, privacy policy, and trademark/copyright information.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory notice section stating that before using the product or this information, readers should review the Notices section on page 27. Includes copyright information for Merative US L.P. (2012, 2024).

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Front matter and start of Product overview for Cúram 8.1.2. Covers edition/copyright, table of contents, and the introductory description of Cúram for social program service delivery. Explains the Cúram architecture: the foundational platform, social programs data model, integrated case management, intake, eligibility and entitlement, evidence management, participant management, and application modules. Also introduces module examples such as Appeals, Business Intelligence and Analytics, and Evidence Broker, with platform capabilities like Intelligent Evidence Gathering, Decision Assist, Financial Management, and Funded Program Management.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-24
children.summary: Pages 15–24 cover the Cúram 8.1.2 product overview and technical architecture. They describe major modules (Outcome Management, Provider Management, Social Enterprise Collaboration, Universal Access, Verification Engine, Income Support, Medical Assistance, Child Welfare), then explain the model-driven Java EE development environment, runtime tiers, and core Java technologies used. The section also notes tablet support recommendations and begins a long deprecated functionality list across versions 8.1 to 7.0.0, including inline action menus, password reset APIs, Flex widgets, evidence broker, and other removed or deprecated features.

children.title: Edition — pages 25-26
children.summary: This section lists deprecated Cúram features and versions, including legacy employment contribution entities, non-identical evidence mapping, over/underpayments graphical view, SEC Case & Participant Index, spell checker, Flex BI charts, IEG 1, and short name substitution, with notes on replacements or removal. It then introduces “Removed functionality” for Cúram 8.1.0, advising upgraders to check Release Notes and use the Artifact Dependencies Search Tool to find dependencies, and begins the PDF format topic with links to prebuilt PDF files.

Front matter for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 and the Product overview. Covers the Cúram architecture and platform: social programs data model, integrated case management, intake, eligibility/entitlement, evidence and participant management, plus application modules such as Appeals, BI&A, and Evidence Broker. Also summarizes technical architecture and ADE/runtime tiers, tablet support, deprecated functionality by release, removed functionality in 8.1.0, and PDF format access/prebuilt PDFs.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: Provides legal notices for the document: sample data and programs are fictitious, sample code may be copied/modified/distributed under the Merative Client Agreement for API-conforming development, but is provided “as is” without warranty or liability. Includes the Merative privacy policy link, trademark attributions for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft, UNIX, and a copyright notice.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: States that all example names in the document are fictitious and that sample programs are provided by Merative under the Client Agreement, with an “AS IS” warranty disclaimer. Includes a privacy policy link. Lists trademark notices for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft/Windows, and UNIX, and notes other names may be trademarks of their owners.

Legal notices and usage permissions for the publication. Covers personal and enterprise use, preservation of proprietary notices, limits on distribution/derivative works, export-law compliance, and Merative’s right to revoke permissions. Includes strong “as-is” warranty and liability disclaimers, notes on patents and third-party product information, disclaimer for external websites, and copyright notice.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for the Cúram 8.1.2 document, listing front matter and major sections. It includes Product overview with subsections on Cúram architecture, technical overview, tablet support, deprecated/removed functionality, and PDF format, plus Notices, Privacy policy, and Trademarks. Useful for locating topics and navigating to relevant chapters.

children.title: 1 Product overview
1.1 Cúram architecture
Cúram Platform
Integrated Case Management
children.summary: This section explains Cúram’s integrated case management capabilities within the platform. It covers intake workflows (triage, screening, application, referrals), eligibility and entitlement management, and evidence management across the full lifecycle. Key themes include supporting “No Wrong Door” intake, cross-program and reusable evidence, one-time evidence capture, household and assistance unit handling, over/underpayment calculations, and business-user tools for policy implementation.

Cúram Platform describes a configurable business and technology platform for health and social programs, with prebuilt components for evidence gathering, decision support, financial management, funded program management, and supervisor workspaces. It emphasizes a social programs data model that captures household data once and reuses it across multiple programs. The section also covers integrated case management: intake (triage, screening, application, referrals), eligibility and entitlement, and evidence management across its lifecycle, including policy tools, cross-program reuse, over/underpayment handling, and No Wrong Door intake.

children.title: Cúram Application Modules
children.summary: This section introduces Cúram application modules as configurable, reusable foundations for common social program business processes. It lists major modules and their purposes, including Income Support, Income Support for Medical Assistance, Child Welfare, Appeals, Business Intelligence and Analytics, Evidence Broker, Outcome Management, Provider Management, Social Enterprise Collaboration, Universal Access, and Verification Engine. It also explains participant management and shared evidence concepts. Open this node to compare core product capabilities and identify which modules support specific program needs.

Cúram architecture describes the product as a foundational platform plus extra application modules. The platform includes configurable components for evidence gathering, decision support, financial and funded program management, supervisor workspaces, and a shared social programs data model. It explains integrated case management for intake, eligibility/entitlement, and evidence lifecycle management, including No Wrong Door intake and over/underpayment handling. The section also introduces participant management and lists major modules such as Income Support, Appeals, BI/Analytics, Evidence Broker, Provider Management, Universal Access, Verification Engine, and others.

children.title: 1.2 Technical overview
The Cúram Application Development Environment (ADE)
children.summary: This section introduces the Cúram Application Development Environment (ADE), which supports building Java EE-compliant applications for social enterprises. It outlines ADE features: n-tier development aids, business infrastructure for eligibility/entitlement rules, a UML-based application model, commercial components, and a pre-built reference application. It also states the development environment’s goals: reduce non-business-specific work so developers can focus on business problems, and use model-based development starting from a platform-independent UML application model.

children.title: Runtime architecture
children.summary: Explains Cúram’s runtime architecture for online client/server deployment. It frames applications as platform-independent at design time, then mapped to a Java EE-style n-tier runtime with separate presentation, application, and persistence tiers. Describes the client-side HTML/JavaScript browser UI, server-side JSP-based presentation layer, HTTP/SSL communication, and RMI-IIOP between presentation and business logic. Also notes that non-browser clients can use generated Server Access Beans.

children.title: Development architecture
children.summary: Describes Cúram’s development architecture and toolchain. The server side uses a model-based, UML-driven approach in the Server Development Environment (SDEJ), with code generators producing entity/process objects plus Remote Interface, Data Access, and Business Object layers. The client side (CDEJ) generates HTML/JSP-based UIs from XML UIM definitions and style sheets, with validation and widget behavior driven by the application model. Also explains the main Java technologies used and how metadata supports UI generation and localization.

children.title: Business infrastructure
children.summary: Cúram’s business infrastructure includes the Cúram Express Rules (CER) environment and the Eligibility and Entitlement Engine. CER lets technical and business users define questions, answer types, and rules via the CER Rules Editor, supporting complex rule sets and display rules. The Eligibility and Entitlement Engine uses CER plus case evidence to assess and reassess eligibility/entitlement over time, accounting for legislative, rate, and circumstance changes.

This section gives a high-level technical overview of Cúram’s architecture and development model. It covers the ADE for building Java EE-compliant social enterprise applications, emphasizing model-based UML development, code generation, and reusable infrastructure. It explains the runtime online client/server architecture (browser UI, JSP presentation tier, RMI-IIOP to business logic, relational database back end), the SDEJ/CDEJ development toolchain, and the business infrastructure for Cúram Express Rules and eligibility/entitlement processing.

children.title: 1.3 Tablet support
children.summary: The Cúram application is certified for tablet use. It recommends a supported browser, 1366x768 minimum resolution, Normal DPI, an ~8-inch or larger device, a 1024x768 minimum viewport, landscape orientation, an external keyboard for heavy data entry, and a reliable network connection. Accessibility is certified for VoiceOver on iOS with an external keyboard. It also notes that Android and iOS do not support Microsoft Word integration technologies.

children.title: 1.4 Deprecated functionality
children.summary: Lists Cúram features deprecated across versions 8.1 through 6.0.0, with brief notes on replacements or removal timelines. Covers deprecated action-menu inline toggles, multi-locale superseded APIs/UIMs, GSS artifacts, IEG currency variables, ClientAccess, password reset APIs, date-picker JSP, batch process replacements, multiple Flex widgets, evidence broker, FILE_EDIT applet, BI/analytics ETLs, CPM features, evidence flow, legacy employment entities, SEC CPI, spell checker, older IEG, and short name substitution. Useful for checking upgrade impact and deprecation history.

children.title: 1.5 Removed functionality
children.summary: Explains that Cúram 8.1.2 includes removed functionality for users upgrading from earlier versions. It directs readers to the Release Notes for details and recommends using the Artifact Dependencies Search Tool from Cúram Support to check whether an implementation depends on removed features. It notes that removed features from Cúram 8.1.0 are listed alphabetically in the following sections.

children.title: 1.6 PDF format
children.summary: Explains how to access Cúram online content in PDF format and points to prebuilt PDF files in the Cúram PDF library. The section then contains legal notices: permissions for personal and commercial use, restrictions on redistribution and derivative works, export compliance, warranty disclaimers, sample code licensing, privacy policy link, and trademark attributions. Useful mainly for users needing PDF access or legal/use terms.

Overview of Cúram 8.1.2 for social-enterprise service delivery. Introduces the platform as a configurable foundation for human services, health, labor, social security, and military/veterans programs, supporting Beveridge and Bismarck models. Covers core architecture and platform capabilities: social programs data model, intelligent evidence gathering, decision assist, financial/funded program management, supervisor workspace, integrated intake, eligibility/entitlement, evidence management, participant management, and major modules such as Appeals, BI/Analytics, Evidence Broker, Provider Management, Universal Access, Verification Engine, and Income Support. Also includes deprecated/removed features, tablet support, PDF access, and legal notices.
